findings.claim: RESOLVED (PR #15, was PRES-001 BLOCKER): mid-run BrokerFetchError previously vaporized already-journaled events; the full event list is now preserved and printed, the failing candidate carries the infra reason, remaining candidates are skipped, exit 2.
RESOLVED (PR #15, was PRES-002 CRITICAL): POST transport errors now surface submission-uncertain distinct from network-failure, so ambiguous broker acceptance is visible for reconciliation.

RESOLVED (PR #15, was PREL-001 CRITICAL): running caps projection now advances only on submitted/already-submitted acknowledgements; rejected orders no longer consume position slots or deployed capital.
RESOLVED (PR #15, was PREL-002 CRITICAL): last_equity of zero or less now halts with kill-switch per the fail-closed spec scenario; the drawdown gate can no longer silently disable itself.
RESOLVED (PR #15, was PREL-003/PREL-004/PRES-003): already-submitted event id includes broker_order_id per design; zero-ATR path characterized; buying power depletes in the running projection.

OPEN follow-up (was PRISK-001): client_order_id hashes rule_version, so a rule-version bump re-orders already-executed days — dedup is per-rule-version, not per-symbol/day. Explicit decision required before any live-trading slice (operational deploy rule vs broker-side same-symbol/day guard). Engram topic trading-system/order-idempotency-scope.

OPEN follow-ups (was PREAD-001..007): duplicated EventBase/ExecutionEventBase definitions; three divergent CLI failure output shapes across entrypoints; BrokerFetchError free-form reason strings vs StrEnum idiom; intent_id_or_symbol parameter naming; duplicated parser boilerplate; repeated skip-construction sites; fetch_main uses module-private _UniversePayload.
